adapter_patterns.definition: Separate validated query transformation, transport extraction, and normalized result transformation behind a provider-neutral port.
One adapter-owned session per bounded call or adapter instance; never a strategy-owned SDK client or ambient global credential.
Require finite connect/read timeouts, typed rate-limit errors, capped exponential backoff with deterministic test hooks, and no retry around access controls.
Validate provider query and normalized result objects with strict Pydantic models; reject unknown fields and empty/stale results.
Preserve source timezone and raw timestamp metadata while deriving explicit UTC availability timestamps; never silently strip provenance.
Keep delivery evidence, normalized point-in-time values, revisions, and corporate actions in separate typed channels only after rights authorize storage.
No cache or immutable snapshot is created until exact storage, backup, retention, and deletion rights pass; cache keys bind provider, request, entitlement hash, and source version.

Allowlisted metadata and hashes may cross the boundary; credentials, account identifiers, provider bodies, cookies, crumbs, URLs with secrets, and raw response excerpts may not enter artifacts, logs, or exceptions.
Use deterministic synthetic HTTP metadata and scrubbed fixtures to test timeouts, retries, rate limits, schema drift, revisions, redaction, and fail-closed rights gates without provider observations.
Data-access plumbing may emit typed observations or text features only; no LLM-originated trade decision, position size, order, or execution path is permitted.
Keep Yahoo/yfinance absent from dependencies and runtime until exact current terms, product scope, intended use, storage, attribution, and retention rights independently verify.

block_reason: No authenticated RTDSM rights-clarification response or independently verified authority and exact-scope evidence was supplied.
disclaimer: Evidence-intake and open-source feasibility only; not legal advice, a rights grant, provider selection, data acquisition, ingestion, research, performance, strategy, risk approval, execution authority, an order, or investment advice. Requester authority is personal and does not represent PwC or any provider rights holder.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: SQLite caches for timezone/cookie/ISIN metadata and in-memory response caching; persistent market-data rights remain separate.
source_evidence.corporate_action_revision: Can return dividends, splits, and capital gains; repair logic may alter results and must not be adopted as authoritative revision semantics.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Temporary cache directories, singleton reset, immutable-argument cache tests, and unit tests; some upstream tests access live services.
source_evidence.error_sanitization: Typed errors exist, but upstream bodies and ticker-specific details may reach logs unless independently bounded.
source_evidence.provider_abstraction: Central YfData singleton and call-level Ticker/history facades; useful as a session-ownership reference, not a provider-neutral contract.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: One shared session/cookie/crumb owner across threads, with caller session validation and locks.
source_evidence.rights_warning: README says yfinance is unaffiliated with Yahoo, points to Yahoo terms, and states the Finance API is intended for personal use only. Apache-2.0 covers software, not Yahoo data.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: DataFrame parsing, metadata checks, empty/error classification, optional repair, and multi-ticker alignment; not a strict point-in-time schema contract.
source_evidence.timeout_retry_backoff_rate_limit: Per-call timeout defaults to 10 seconds; explicit rate-limit exception; transient request retry exists, while project README does not grant Yahoo data rights.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Parses exchange timezone, converts Unix timestamps through UTC, and optionally strips timezone on combined daily data.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Yahoo product scope, continuing availability, point-in-time fitness, accuracy, storage, and intended-use rights remain RIGHTS_UNVERIFIED.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: Test recordings and provider behavior vary; persistence policy is not a substitute for provider rights.
source_evidence.corporate_action_revision: Historical fetcher exposes optional actions, but no general authoritative revision/delisting guarantee follows from the abstraction.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Provider contract tests and scrubbed VCR recordings demonstrate bounded fixture-driven testing, subject to license and data-rights review.
source_evidence.error_sanitization: VCR configuration scrubs cookies, crumbs, headers, dates, and response fragments; useful as a test-sanitization reference.
source_evidence.provider_abstraction: Strong provider registry plus transform_query/extract_data/transform_data fetcher lifecycle and standard models.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: Provider helpers own transport details; query executor filters SecretStr credentials before dispatch.
source_evidence.rights_warning: AGPL software rights do not grant rights to any connected provider data; adaptations must be independently implemented or comply with AGPL obligations.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: Pydantic query/result models and model_validate at the provider boundary are directly reusable as an independently implemented pattern.
source_evidence.timeout_retry_backoff_rate_limit: Behavior is provider-specific rather than uniformly guaranteed; the pattern requires an explicit neutral transport policy before reuse.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Provider adapters normalize dates/timezones; the inspected Yahoo helper can remove timezone information, which is unsuitable for an audit source of truth without preserving raw timestamps.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: No inspected abstraction establishes RTDSM or Yahoo rights, source authority, or point-in-time fitness.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: SEC helper writes a local text cache; this is an anti-pattern until rights, keys, provenance, and retention are explicit.
source_evidence.corporate_action_revision: Thin yfinance dividend access and filing helpers do not establish revision or corporate-action authority.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: The inspected data utilities lack a comprehensive synthetic transport contract suite.
source_evidence.error_sanitization: Some errors and URLs can include provider context; credential-bearing URLs and broad exception strings are patterns to reject.
source_evidence.provider_abstraction: Utility classes group vendor calls, but clients and credentials are often global and not provider-neutral.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: Mostly implicit library/global clients; direct requests calls do not demonstrate bounded session ownership.
source_evidence.rights_warning: Software license does not grant vendor-data rights; project data utilities must not be treated as evidence of lawful or accurate access.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: Returns DataFrames, strings, and dictionaries without a shared strict boundary schema.
source_evidence.timeout_retry_backoff_rate_limit: Inspected direct requests calls generally omit explicit timeout, retry, backoff, and rate-limit controls.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Date parameters exist, but no uniform UTC availability-time contract is evident in the inspected utilities.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Use only as an anti-pattern inventory for globals, unbounded requests, credential-bearing URLs, and mixed return schemas.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: Per-symbol CSV cache with a same-day TTL and stale-cache tests; historical immutability assumptions must not be copied blindly.
source_evidence.corporate_action_revision: Yahoo plumbing does not establish authoritative corporate-action or vintage revision handling.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Synthetic monkeypatch tests cover cache freshness, stale rows, routing, and configuration isolation.
source_evidence.error_sanitization: Typed error classes are reusable conceptually, but some functions return or print exception text and must not be copied.
source_evidence.provider_abstraction: Category/tool registry routes to vendor implementations with typed behavioral errors and fallback chains.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: Vendor modules own calls; no single neutral session contract is enforced across providers.
source_evidence.rights_warning: Study data-access plumbing only. Do not copy any LLM trade-decision, position-size, or order design. Yahoo rights remain unverified.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: OHLCV cleanup, stale-data rejection, symbol normalization, and error taxonomy are useful plumbing references, not authoritative data schemas.
source_evidence.timeout_retry_backoff_rate_limit: Explicit Alpha Vantage timeout, typed rate-limit errors, and yfinance exponential backoff; retry lacks jitter and sleeps synchronously.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Filters rows to the requested date but sometimes strips timezone; a future adapter must preserve UTC availability and source timezone metadata.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Fallback retrieval is not proof of lawful use, accuracy, point-in-time fitness, or provider continuity.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: Persistent storage rights are not resolved.
source_evidence.provider_abstraction: Official product description and file-download surface.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: Not documented as an API/session contract.
source_evidence.rights_warning: Public availability and research examples do not resolve persistent automated-model rights.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: Describes vintage columns and release-value files, not an application schema.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Monthly update cadence is described; exact availability timestamps are not granted as an API contract.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Authority, exact series/delivery/account scope, storage, derived use, retention, and revocation remain unresolved.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: Research use is stated, but copyrighted third-party content may require owner permission.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Content SHA-256 preserves the inspected snapshot identity.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: Excessive or disruptive access may be restricted.
source_evidence.rights_warning: Informational, educational, and research use language is not an authenticated exact-scope grant for RTDSM automation or storage.
source_evidence.timeout_retry_backoff_rate_limit: No exact automated access limits are specified.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Page states last update 2026-04-01; snapshot captured in UTC.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Provider authority and BLS-originated content rights remain unresolved.

source_evidence.corporate_action_revision: Documents BLS seasonal-factor revisions incorporated into February vintages.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Content SHA-256 identifies the inspected PDF.
source_evidence.provider_abstraction: Official metadata documentation for RTDSM PCPI.
source_evidence.rights_warning: Confirms BLS origin but does not establish upstream rights coverage.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: Identifies PCPI as seasonally adjusted CPI and documents revisions.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Describes monthly vintages; no entitlement timing contract.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Separate BLS/rightsholder permission question remains open.

source_evidence.corporate_action_revision: Revision methodology is central and explicitly documented.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Content SHA-256 identifies the inspected PDF.
source_evidence.rights_warning: Methodology and research suitability do not grant operational rights.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: Documents vintage layout, transformations, and caveats.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Uses dated vintages; no automated availability API contract.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Exact requested-series scope and current rights remain unresolved.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: Storage/use provisions do not resolve yfinance's exact Finance data surface for this project.
source_evidence.corporate_action_revision: No authoritative market-history or corporate-action guarantee.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Content SHA-256 identifies the inspected terms snapshot.
source_evidence.provider_abstraction: General Yahoo API terms, not a confirmed Yahoo Finance market-data product entitlement.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: Terms require applicable account/API documents and permit discretionary rate limits.
source_evidence.rights_warning: Revocable general API terms and software/API access rights do not grant rights to all Yahoo Finance data.
source_evidence.timeout_retry_backoff_rate_limit: Rate limits may be imposed at Yahoo's discretion; no yfinance-specific limits are established.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Terms can change by posting; currentness must be revalidated.
source_evidence.unresolved_limitations: Exact current Yahoo Finance terms, product scope, and intended-use rights remain RIGHTS_UNVERIFIED.

source_evidence.caching_persistence: Ownership/reuse restrictions require exact product terms or written permission.
source_evidence.deterministic_testing: Content SHA-256 identifies the inspected terms snapshot.
source_evidence.request_session_ownership: Personal, revocable service/API license subject to product-specific terms.
source_evidence.rights_warning: General terms do not prove that yfinance retrieval is authorized for storage, modeling, backtesting, or redistribution.
source_evidence.schema_validation_normalization: No data accuracy or point-in-time fitness guarantee.
source_evidence.timeout_retry_backoff_rate_limit: High-volume activity may require prior written consent.
source_evidence.timestamp_timezone: Terms snapshot is current only as of the research timestamp.

transition_rules.rule: Missing or ambiguous responder identity, role, authority basis, rights-holding entity, authenticated provenance, or independent verification keeps the aggregate blocked.
Every Phase 24 question must have mutually consistent PASS or enforceable CONDITIONAL evidence before rights can verify.
Every exact product, series, delivery, party, account, user, environment, access, storage, use, output, attribution, retention, and revocation scope element must satisfy.
CONDITIONAL satisfies only when every condition has an enforceable fail-closed control, a portable acceptance test identifier, and a passed test.
Any FAIL or MISSING question or scope element keeps acquisition, storage, ingestion, research, and operational composition blocked.
Every cited evidence identifier must resolve to an independently verified metadata record with an immutable SHA-256 and authenticated provenance.
A change to terms, product, series, delivery, licensed party, entitlement, users, use, rights holder, expiry, or revocation state requires full revalidation.
Requester identity, employer, title, repository ownership, commit, PR approval, credential possession, or successful retrieval is never provider-rights evidence.
A verified response may only support a separately authorized acquisition decision; Phase 25 never activates an adapter or fetches observations.
